summary: Tested Tip-the-Forger Stripe payment feature. 8/11 new tips tests pass. CRITICAL backend bug: GET /api/tips/status/{session_id} returns 500 because emergentintegrations Stripe proxy cannot find sessions it just created via the same key. Regression: all 27 prior backend tests still pass. Frontend tip flow works end-to-end through Stripe redirect, including `?tip_cancelled=1` toast handling.

critical.issue: Returns 500 InternalServerError. Underlying error from emergentintegrations: 'Failed to retrieve session status: No such checkout.session: <id>'. The session was created successfully via the same StripeCheckout(api_key='sk_test_emergent') instance (proxy base https://integrations.emergentagent.com/stripe), but stripe.checkout.Session.retrieve() against the same proxy returns 'No such checkout.session'. Reproducible with a standalone create+retrieve script (see RCA). This breaks the entire frontend polling-after-redirect flow: a paid tip would NEVER be confirmed via /tips/status, only via the webhook. _credit_tip() never runs from the status path, so tips_count/tips_total_cents would not update if Stripe webhook isn't actually delivered to this preview URL.

minor.issue: origin_url is taken from the client; a malicious client could redirect users to attacker-controlled domains via success_url/cancel_url. Consider validating against an allow-list or deriving origin from request headers.

No webhook_secret is configured; emergentintegrations.handle_webhook is called without a signing secret. Signature is effectively unverified. Works (returns 400 on bad signature in our test), but in production Stripe events could be spoofed.
Amount is sent as a float (e.g. 2.0) — fine for now but consider cents/integer to avoid float rounding.

integration_issues.flow: Tip status polling after Stripe success redirect
integration_issues.issue: Because /api/tips/status/{sid} returns 500, the polling loop in MovieDetail.jsx will exhaust 6 attempts silently and never show the success toast / never refresh tip-stats. Currently the catch swallows the error, so the user sees no feedback after a real payment. Consider surfacing a fallback error after final attempt.

action_items: Investigate emergent Stripe proxy: sessions created via sk_test_emergent at https://integrations.emergentagent.com/stripe are not retrievable via the same key. Likely needs a different retrieve endpoint, or upgrading emergentintegrations. Consider using the websearch tool for emergent Stripe integration retrieval/status documentation.
Add a final error toast / fallback UI in MovieDetail.jsx polling loop so users aren't left without feedback when /tips/status fails.
Tighten origin_url validation in /api/tips/checkout to avoid open-redirect via Stripe success/cancel URLs.
Set STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET and pass it to StripeCheckout so webhook signature is actually verified.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~650 lines — split into routers (auth, movies, ai, tips, seed).

_credit_tip writes both into payment_transactions and into movies + tips collections without a Mongo transaction; if the inserts partially succeed (e.g. movie update OK but tips insert fails) state becomes inconsistent. Wrap in a session or set credited=True AFTER both writes.
tips_status is a public GET — anyone with a session_id can trigger a Stripe API call and (if proxy worked) a DB write. Consider rate-limiting or requiring auth.
TIP_PACKAGES stored as floats (2.00, 5.00). Use cents-int throughout backend; convert to dollars only for display.
GET /api/movies/{mid}/tips exposes tipper_id without masking. Consider returning only username/avatar to avoid leaking user IDs.
test_credentials: Tests register fresh users dynamically. Pattern test_tip_<hex>@cinemaforge.io / Pass1234!. UI test viewer: test_uitip_<hex>@cinemaforge.io.
seed_data_creation: Reused existing idempotent /api/seed (12 curator movies). One creator-owned + one viewer-owned test movie were created during the tips tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_tips.py. Key new endpoints: GET /api/tips/packages (4 fixed: spark $2, ember $5, blaze $10, inferno $25), POST /api/tips/checkout (auth required, validates package, rejects self-tip with 400 'You can't tip your own forge'), GET /api/tips/status/{sid} (BROKEN — 500), POST /api/webhook/stripe (handles bad sig as 400), GET /api/movies/{id}/tips (count/total/tips). Frontend data-testids: tip-btn, tip-dialog, tip-close, tip-packages, tip-pkg-{spark|ember|blaze|inferno}, tip-confirm, tip-stats. The Stripe redirect URL works (https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_...). DO NOT complete payment in browser. Webhook secret is NOT configured.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: Run standalone script `StripeCheckout(api_key='sk_test_emergent').create_checkout_session(...)` then immediately `get_checkout_status(returned_session_id)`. Create returns a valid `cs_test_...` ID and a `checkout.stripe.com` URL, but retrieve raises `stripe._error.InvalidRequestError: No such checkout.session: cs_test_...`. Library at /root/.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/emergentintegrations/payments/stripe/checkout.py sets stripe.api_base='https://integrations.emergentagent.com/stripe' when the key contains 'sk_test_emergent'. Both create and retrieve should hit the proxy, but only create succeeds. Likely the emergent proxy supports POST /checkout/sessions but not GET /checkout/sessions/{id} for the test key, OR session IDs created via proxy aren't retrievable via the same path. Mitigation paths: (a) wait for fixed emergentintegrations release supporting retrieval, (b) catch the CheckoutError in tips_status and return last known DB status (payment_status='initiated') so polling stops gracefully, (c) rely on webhook delivery instead of status polling for crediting.
